query: Florida Legislature Special Session E May 2026 latest news today May 20 conference committee progress
answer:
The research agent returned extensive findings. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# Florida Legislature Special Session E (2026E) — Status Report as of May 20, 2026

## Overview

**Special Session E** is an **18-day budget-only special session** running **May 12–May 29, 2026**. It was convened after the regular 2026 legislative session ended in mid-March without a budget deal — an unusual failure attributed to intra-party dysfunction within the GOP-controlled Legislature. As of May 20, the session is at its **midpoint** with **9 days remaining** and **no final conference agreement reached**.

This is Florida's **fifth special session designation of 2026** (Sessions A through E).

---

## Conference Committee Progress

The **Appropriations Budget Conference** was appointed on **May 12** (opening day) and is organized into **seven subcommittees**, all chaired by senators:

| Subcommittee | Chair |
|---|---|
| Budget (At Large) | Sen. Hooper |
| Agriculture, Environment & General Gov't | Sen. Brodeur |
| Criminal and Civil Justice | Sen. Garcia |
| Health and Human Services | Sen. Trumbull |
| Higher Education | Sen. Harrell |
| Pre-K–12 Education | Sen. Burgess |
| Transportation, Tourism & Econ. Development | Sen. DiCeglie |

**Multiple rounds of offers** have been exchanged (House Offer 1, House Offer 2, Senate Offer 1, Senate Offer 2). The **~$1.45 billion gap** between the two chambers is still being negotiated. **No conference report has been filed or adopted as of May 20.**

Under Florida law, a **72-hour "cooling off" period** is required between the conference report's release and a final floor vote, meaning an agreement must be reached by approximately **May 25–26** to allow votes before the May 29 deadline.

---

## Budget Numbers

| Version | Total |
|---|---|
| **HB 5001-E (House)** | **$113.6 billion** |
| **SB 2500-E (Senate)** | **$115.0 billion** |
| **Gap** | **~$1.45 billion** |
| Agreed General Revenue Framework | $51.98 billion |
| Governor's Original Proposal | ~$117 billion |

Both chambers' proposals represent a **slight reduction** from the FY 2025-26 enacted budget (~$116.5B) and are significantly below the Governor's request.

---

## Votes Already Taken (May 12 — Opening Day)

| Chamber | Bill | Result |
|---|---|---|
| **House** | HB 5001-E | **Passed 98-8** |
| **Senate** | SB 2500-E | **Passed 32-0** |

Both chambers then acceded to a Budget Conference request the same day.

---

## Major Sticking Points

| Issue | House | Senate |
|---|---|---|
| **Florida Forever (conservation)** | $25M | $75M (Gov. requested $115M) |
| **Rural & Family Lands** | $75M | $0 |
| **Florida State Guard** | Proposed eliminating funding | — |
| **Healthcare** | Contested (HMO cuts, nursing home rates) | Contested |
| **Higher Education** | Lower funding | Higher funding |
| **State Employee Pay** | Contested | No health insurance cost hikes |
| **Gas Tax Pause** | Under discussion | Under discussion |

Governor DeSantis signaled on **May 19** that **property tax reform** will likely require yet another special session beyond Session E.

---

## Key Deadlines

- **May 25–26**: Effective deadline for conference agreement (to allow 72-hour cooling period)
- **May 29**: Scheduled adjournment
- **July 1, 2026**: Start of FY 2026-27

If the Legislature fails to pass a budget by May 29, **yet another special session** would be required before July 1.

---

## Immigration Note

Special Session E is **exclusively budget-focused**. Immigration legislation is **not** part of Session E's scope. Any immigration enforcement funding would be embedded within general appropriations rather than standalone bills.

---

## Political Context

- **Key leaders**: Senate President Ben Albritton, House Speaker Daniel Perez, Governor Ron DeSantis
- Politico reported **"distrust and dysfunction"** within the GOP-controlled Legislature as a major factor
- DeSantis criticized lawmakers' **"personal agendas"**; some Republicans questioned DeSantis' special session priorities
- Senate President Albritton's signature **"Rural Renaissance"** initiative was largely dropped

key_facts: Florida Special Session E (2026E) is an 18-day budget-only session running May 12–29, 2026, convened after the regular session ended in mid-March without a budget deal.
As of May 20, 2026 (session midpoint), no conference report has been filed or adopted; 9 days remain.
The Appropriations Budget Conference was appointed on May 12 with seven subcommittees, all chaired by senators.
Multiple rounds of offers have been exchanged (House Offer 1 & 2, Senate Offer 1 & 2), but a ~$1.45 billion gap remains between chambers.
The House budget (HB 5001-E) totals ~$113.6B; the Senate budget (SB 2500-E) totals ~$115.0B.
Both chambers passed their budget versions on opening day: House 98-8, Senate 32-0.
A 72-hour cooling-off period is required before final vote, meaning a deal must be reached by ~May 25-26 to meet the May 29 adjournment deadline.

Major sticking points include Florida Forever conservation funding ($25M House vs $75M Senate), Rural & Family Lands ($75M House vs $0 Senate), Florida State Guard funding, healthcare, higher education, and state employee pay.
Governor DeSantis signaled on May 19 that property tax reform will likely require a separate future special session.
Session E is exclusively budget-focused; immigration legislation is not part of its scope.
This is Florida's fifth special session designation of 2026 (Sessions A through E).
If no budget passes by May 29, yet another special session would be needed before the July 1 fiscal year start.
